title: Spanning Tree Protocol (STP)
description: Prevents Layer 2 loops in redundant switch topologies by blocking redundant paths.
fullExplanation: Without STP, redundant switch links create broadcast storms — a broadcast frame loops forever, consuming all bandwidth. STP (IEEE 802.1D) prevents this by electing a Root Bridge and blocking all redundant paths. Only one active path exists between any two switches. If the active path fails, STP unblocks a redundant path (convergence takes 30-50 seconds in classic STP). RSTP (802.1w) converges in 1-2 seconds. MSTP (802.1s) runs multiple STP instances for different VLANs.

portStates.description: Receives BPDUs but doesn't forward frames. Prevents loops.
Processes BPDUs, participates in election. 15 seconds.
Builds MAC table but doesn't forward frames. 15 seconds.
Fully operational — forwards frames and learns MACs.

portRoles.description: Best path toward Root Bridge. One per non-root switch.
Best port on each segment toward Root Bridge. Forwards traffic.
Redundant port — blocked to prevent loops.

versions.feature: Multiple instances — different topology per VLAN group.

keyFacts: Root Bridge elected by lowest Bridge ID (priority + MAC address)
PortFast: skip Listening/Learning for access ports (hosts, not switches)
BPDU Guard: disable port if BPDU received on PortFast port (prevents rogue switches)
Root Guard: prevent port from becoming root port (protect root bridge position)
Loop Guard: prevent port from transitioning to forwarding if BPDUs stop
STP manipulation attack: attacker sends superior BPDUs to become root bridge
